forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,104 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a080
+project: spatial-nav
+title: 'ARCHITECTURE FIX: collapse Focusable into FocusScope (three peers, not four)'
+---
+## What
+
+The current architecture has **four React/Rust peer types** for focus: `Focusable`, `FocusZone`, `FocusLayer`, and a composite `FocusScope` that wraps either `<Focusable>` or `<FocusZone>` and adds CommandScope. This was wrong. The composite-vs-primitive split smears `showFocusBar`, click handling, and indicator rendering across two layers and is the systemic root of why the user can't see focus on anything.
+
+The correct model is **three peer types**:
+
+| Concept | Role | React | Rust |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| Layer | Modal boundary, hard nav stop | `<FocusLayer>` | `FocusLayer` |
+| Zone | Navigable container | `<FocusZone>` | `FocusZone` |
+| Scope | Leaf â€” shows focus, takes clicks, navigates | `<FocusScope>` | `FocusScope` |
+
+`FocusScope` is the leaf. It does what the pre-spatial-nav `<FocusScope>` already did: render the focus indicator, handle clicks, route to the command scope chain. There is **no separate `<Focusable>` component** and no composite layer.
+
+## What changes
+
+### React side
+
+- **Delete `<Focusable>`.** Its responsibilities (mint SpatialKey, call `spatial_register_focusable`, render `<FocusIndicator>`, handle click â†’ `spatial_focus`, subscribe to claim events) move into `<FocusScope>` directly.
+- **`<FocusScope>` becomes the leaf primitive** (no longer composite, no longer `kind` prop).
+- **`<FocusZone>` keeps its current shape** â€” it's already the container primitive, no changes beyond renaming any `Focusable` references in its docstrings/types.
+- **`<FocusLayer>` keeps its current shape**.
+- All `<Focusable>` call sites become `<FocusScope>` call sites â€” but most of them already say `<FocusScope>` in the codebase, so this is mostly a "stop the indirection" change rather than a rename across hundreds of files.
+- `kind="zone"` prop on `<FocusScope>` goes away. Use `<FocusZone>` directly when a zone is wanted.
+
+### Rust side
+
+- **Rename `Focusable` â†’ `FocusScope`** (the leaf struct).
+- The internal `FocusScope` enum (sum of `Focusable | Zone`) goes away â€” registry stores the two struct types via an internal discriminator, but the public API exposes `FocusScope` as the leaf struct and `FocusZone` as the container struct. No public enum to navigate.
+- Tauri command renames: `spatial_register_focusable` â†’ `spatial_register_scope`. `spatial_register_zone` keeps its name. `spatial_unregister_scope` already correct.
+- `FocusEventSink` / `NavStrategy` traits keep their shapes; only the type they reference changes.
+
+### Cards to update after this lands
+
+Every card in the spatial-nav project that names `Focusable` or describes the composite `FocusScope` model needs a documentation refresh. Canonical terminology section in card `01KNQXW7HH...` is the source of truth and gets updated first. Sweep the others mechanically.
+
+## Why this fixes the focus-not-visible bug
+
+Right now: the composite `<FocusScope>` wraps `<Focusable>`, so a leaf in the codebase actually mounts two stacked components. Each has its own click handler, its own `data-focused` attribute, its own indicator-render decision. When `showFocusBar={false}` is set on the outer composite, it suppresses the *composite's* indicator but the inner `<Focusable>`'s indicator may also be configured separately. The visual result depends on which layer "wins," which depends on subtle prop-flow decisions that vary by call site.
+
+After the collapse: one component, one click handler, one `data-focused`, one indicator render decision. Easy to reason about, easy to test "click â†’ indicator visible" end-to-end.
+
+## Subtasks
+
+- [ ] Inventory every place `<Focusable>` is imported or referenced in `kanban-app/ui/src/`
+- [ ] Move `<Focusable>`'s implementation into `<FocusScope>` (focus-scope.tsx)
+- [ ] Delete `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focusable.tsx`
+- [ ] Drop the `kind` prop from `<FocusScope>`; call sites that need a zone use `<FocusZone>` directly
+- [ ] Rename Rust `Focusable` struct â†’ `FocusScope` (in `swissarmyhammer-focus`)
+- [ ] Drop the Rust `FocusScope` enum (was sum of `Focusable | Zone`); registry stores two distinct types internally
+- [ ] Tauri command `spatial_register_focusable` â†’ `spatial_register_scope`
+- [ ] Update terminology section in card `01KNQXW7HH...` to describe three peers, not four
+- [ ] Sweep every spatial-nav card for `Focusable` references; update doc references
+- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` + `pnpm vitest run` â€” all green
+- [ ] Manual verification: clicking each kind of leaf (column header, card title, status pill, field label, navbar button, perspective tab, grid cell) shows a visible focus indicator
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] No `Focusable` type or component exists in the codebase (Rust or TS)
+- [ ] `<FocusScope>` is the leaf primitive â€” handles registration, click, claim subscription, indicator render, all in one component
+- [ ] `<FocusZone>` is the container primitive (unchanged)
+- [ ] `<FocusLayer>` is the layer primitive (unchanged)
+- [ ] All seven leaf categories (column header, card title, card status, card pills, inspector label, inspector pill, navbar button, perspective tab, grid cell) show a visible focus indicator on click
+- [ ] All existing tests pass after the rename + collapse
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] `focus-scope.test.tsx` â€” scope renders an indicator when its key is the focused key
+- [ ] Per-component "click leaf â†’ indicator visible" tests added to each per-component card
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` clean
+
+## Workflow
+
+This is a refactor with a clear before/after. Sequence:
+
+1. Rust rename first (lowest blast-radius â€” just a type rename + Tauri command rename).
+2. React collapse second (`<Focusable>` impl moves into `<FocusScope>`, file deletion, prop-shape change).
+3. Per-component card sweep last (mechanical doc updates).
+
+After it lands, the per-component focus-visibility cards will have a much smaller surface to debug because there's only one place focus rendering can come from.
+
+## Blocks
+
+This card **blocks** every reopened per-component card:
+- `01KNQXZ81Q` Board view
+- `01KQ20MX70` Column
+- `01KQ20NMRQ` Card
+- `01KNQXZZ9V` Grid view
+- `01KQ20Q2PN` NavBar
+- `01KQ20QW3K` Toolbar
+- `01KPZS32YN` Perspective
+- `01KNQXYC4RB` Inspector layer
+- `01KNQY0P9J` Inspector field rows
+- `01KQ5PEHWT` Release-blocker umbrella
+
+The per-component focus-visibility work is much easier on top of the collapsed architecture, so this card runs first.
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value: ARCHITECTURE FIX: collapse Focusable into FocusScope (three peers, not four)

## What

The current architecture has **four React/Rust peer types** for focus: `Focusable`, `FocusZone`, `FocusLayer`, and a composite `FocusScope` that wraps either `<Focusable>` or `<FocusZone>` and adds CommandScope. This was wrong. The composite-vs-primitive split smears `showFocusBar`, click handling, and indicator rendering across two layers and is the systemic root of why the user can't see focus on anything.

The correct model is **three peer types**:

| Concept | Role | React | Rust |
|---|---|---|---|
| Layer | Modal boundary, hard nav stop | `<FocusLayer>` | `FocusLayer` |
| Zone | Navigable container | `<FocusZone>` | `FocusZone` |
| Scope | Leaf â€” shows focus, takes clicks, navigates | `<FocusScope>` | `FocusScope` |

`FocusScope` is the leaf. It does what the pre-spatial-nav `<FocusScope>` already did: render the focus indicator, handle clicks, route to the command scope chain. There is **no separate `<Focusable>` component** and no composite layer.

## What changes

### React side

- **Delete `<Focusable>`.** Its responsibilities (mint SpatialKey, call `spatial_register_focusable`, render `<FocusIndicator>`, handle click â†’ `spatial_focus`, subscribe to claim events) move into `<FocusScope>` directly.
- **`<FocusScope>` becomes the leaf primitive** (no longer composite, no longer `kind` prop).
- **`<FocusZone>` keeps its current shape** â€” it's already the container primitive, no changes beyond renaming any `Focusable` references in its docstrings/types.
- **`<FocusLayer>` keeps its current shape**.
- All `<Focusable>` call sites become `<FocusScope>` call sites â€” but most of them already say `<FocusScope>` in the codebase, so this is mostly a "stop the indirection" change rather than a rename across hundreds of files.
- `kind="zone"` prop on `<FocusScope>` goes away. Use `<FocusZone>` directly when a zone is wanted.

### Rust side

- **Rename `Focusable` â†’ `FocusScope`** (the leaf struct).
- The internal `FocusScope` enum (sum of `Focusable | Zone`) goes away â€” registry stores the two struct types via an internal discriminator, but the public API exposes `FocusScope` as the leaf struct and `FocusZone` as the container struct. No public enum to navigate.
- Tauri command renames: `spatial_register_focusable` â†’ `spatial_register_scope`. `spatial_register_zone` keeps its name. `spatial_unregister_scope` already correct.
- `FocusEventSink` / `NavStrategy` traits keep their shapes; only the type they reference changes.

### Cards to update after this lands

Every card in the spatial-nav project that names `Focusable` or describes the composite `FocusScope` model needs a documentation refresh. Canonical terminology section in card `01KNQXW7HH...` is the source of truth and gets updated first. Sweep the others mechanically.

## Why this fixes the focus-not-visible bug

Right now: the composite `<FocusScope>` wraps `<Focusable>`, so a leaf in the codebase actually mounts two stacked components. Each has its own click handler, its own `data-focused` attribute, its own indicator-render decision. When `showFocusBar={false}` is set on the outer composite, it suppresses the *composite's* indicator but the inner `<Focusable>`'s indicator may also be configured separately. The visual result depends on which layer "wins," which depends on subtle prop-flow decisions that vary by call site.

After the collapse: one component, one click handler, one `data-focused`, one indicator render decision. Easy to reason about, easy to test "click â†’ indicator visible" end-to-end.

## Subtasks

- [ ] Inventory every place `<Focusable>` is imported or referenced in `kanban-app/ui/src/`
- [ ] Move `<Focusable>`'s implementation into `<FocusScope>` (focus-scope.tsx)
- [ ] Delete `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focusable.tsx`
- [ ] Drop the `kind` prop from `<FocusScope>`; call sites that need a zone use `<FocusZone>` directly
- [ ] Rename Rust `Focusable` struct â†’ `FocusScope` (in `swissarmyhammer-focus`)
- [ ] Drop the Rust `FocusScope` enum (was sum of `Focusable | Zone`); registry stores two distinct types internally
- [ ] Tauri command `spatial_register_focusable` â†’ `spatial_register_scope`
- [ ] Update terminology section in card `01KNQXW7HH...` to describe three peers, not four
- [ ] Sweep every spatial-nav card for `Focusable` references; update doc references
- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` + `pnpm vitest run` â€” all green
- [ ] Manual verification: clicking each kind of leaf (column header, card title, status pill, field label, navbar button, perspective tab, grid cell) shows a visible focus indicator

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No `Focusable` type or component exists in the codebase (Rust or TS)
- [ ] `<FocusScope>` is the leaf primitive â€” handles registration, click, claim subscription, indicator render, all in one component
- [ ] `<FocusZone>` is the container primitive (unchanged)
- [ ] `<FocusLayer>` is the layer primitive (unchanged)
- [ ] All seven leaf categories (column header, card title, card status, card pills, inspector label, inspector pill, navbar button, perspective tab, grid cell) show a visible focus indicator on click
- [ ] All existing tests pass after the rename + collapse

## Tests

- [ ] `focus-scope.test.tsx` â€” scope renders an indicator when its key is the focused key
- [ ] Per-component "click leaf â†’ indicator visible" tests added to each per-component card
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` clean

## Workflow

This is a refactor with a clear before/after. Sequence:

1. Rust rename first (lowest blast-radius â€” just a type rename + Tauri command rename).
2. React collapse second (`<Focusable>` impl moves into `<FocusScope>`, file deletion, prop-shape change).
3. Per-component card sweep last (mechanical doc updates).

After it lands, the per-component focus-visibility cards will have a much smaller surface to debug because there's only one place focus rendering can come from.

## Blocks

This card **blocks** every reopened per-component card:
- `01KNQXZ81Q` Board view
- `01KQ20MX70` Column
- `01KQ20NMRQ` Card
- `01KNQXZZ9V` Grid view
- `01KQ20Q2PN` NavBar
- `01KQ20QW3K` Toolbar
- `01KPZS32YN` Perspective
- `01KNQXYC4RB` Inspector layer
- `01KNQY0P9J` Inspector field rows
- `01KQ5PEHWT` Release-blocker umbrella

The per-component focus-visibility work is much easier on top of the collapsed architecture, so this card runs first.

-`FocusScope` is the leaf. It does what the pre-spatial-nav `<FocusScope>` already did: render the focus indicator, handle clicks, route to the command scope chain. There is **no separate `<Focusable>` component** and no composite layer.
+`FocusScope` is the leaf. It does what the pre-spatial-nav `<FocusScope>` already did: render the focus indicator, handle clicks, route to the command scope chain. There is **no separate `<Focusable>` component** in the final state, and no composite layer.
+
+## CRITICAL ORDERING â€” `<Focusable>` stays as a re-export until the end
+
+This card **must NOT delete `<Focusable>`**. The per-component cards (column, card, navbar, etc.) need a green build during their TDD loops, and many of them currently reference `<Focusable>` from inside their own component code AND inside their tests. If `<Focusable>` disappears here, every per-component card breaks before it can TDD anything.
+
+What this card does:
+1. Move `<Focusable>`'s implementation into `<FocusScope>` so `<FocusScope>` is the leaf primitive.
+2. Reduce `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focusable.tsx` to a one-line re-export: `export { FocusScope as Focusable } from "./focus-scope"`. (Type alias if needed: `export type FocusableProps = FocusScopeProps`.)
+3. Add a `@deprecated` JSDoc comment on the re-export pointing at `<FocusScope>` and the eventual deletion card `01KQ5PSMYE...`.
+
+The actual file deletion happens in card `01KQ5PSMYE...` AFTER every per-component card lands. That card runs **last** in the chain, with `depends_on` set to every per-component card + the release-blocker umbrella.

+- **`<FocusScope>` becomes the leaf primitive** â€” no composite, no `kind` prop.
+- **`<Focusable>` becomes a thin re-export** of `<FocusScope>` for transitional compatibility. NOT deleted in this card.
+- **`<FocusZone>` keeps its current shape**; only `Focusable` references in its docstrings/types get renamed.
 - **`<FocusLayer>` keeps its current shape**.
-- All `<Focusable>` call sites become `<FocusScope>` call sites â€” but most of them already say `<FocusScope>` in the codebase, so this is mostly a "stop the indirection" change rather than a rename across hundreds of files.
-- `kind="zone"` prop on `<FocusScope>` goes away. Use `<FocusZone>` directly when a zone is wanted.
+- **Drop `kind="zone"` prop on `<FocusScope>`.** Call sites that need a zone use `<FocusZone>` directly. The transitional re-export of `<Focusable>` does not accept `kind="zone"` (it forwards to `<FocusScope>` which is now leaf-only).

 ### Rust side

+## Why the bridging-as-deprecation pattern matters

-Right now: the composite `<FocusScope>` wraps `<Focusable>`, so a leaf in the codebase actually mounts two stacked components. Each has its own click handler, its own `data-focused` attribute, its own indicator-render decision. When `showFocusBar={false}` is set on the outer composite, it suppresses the *composite's* indicator but the inner `<Focusable>`'s indicator may also be configured separately. +

 ## Acceptance Criteria

-- [ ] No `Focusable` type or component exists in the codebase (Rust or TS)

-- [ ] All existing tests pass after the rename + collapse

+- [ ] Rust `Focusable` struct renamed to `FocusScope`; no public enum; `spatial_register_focusable` â†’ `spatial_register_scope`
+- [ ] All existing tests pass after the rename + collapse â€” if a test references the old `<Focusable>` it still works because of the re-export
+- [ ] No breaking change to call sites: every existing `<Focusable>` usage continues to compile and behave identically

 - [ ] `focus-scope.test.tsx` â€” scope renders an indicator when its key is the focused key

@@ -18,29 +18,18 @@
 | Zone | Navigable container | `<FocusZone>` | `FocusZone` |
 | Scope | Leaf â€” shows focus, takes clicks, navigates | `<FocusScope>` | `FocusScope` |

-`FocusScope` is the leaf. It does what the pre-spatial-nav `<FocusScope>` already did: render the focus indicator, handle clicks, route to the command scope chain. There is **no separate `<Focusable>` component** in the final state, and no composite layer.
+`FocusScope` is the leaf. It does what the pre-spatial-nav `<FocusScope>` already did: render the focus indicator, handle clicks, route to the command scope chain. There is **no separate `<Focusable>` component** and no composite layer.

-## CRITICAL ORDERING â€” `<Focusable>` stays as a re-export until the end
-
-This card **must NOT delete `<Focusable>`**. The per-component cards (column, card, navbar, etc.) need a green build during their TDD loops, and many of them currently reference `<Focusable>` from inside their own component code AND inside their tests. If `<Focusable>` disappears here, every per-component card breaks before it can TDD anything.
-
-What this card does:
-1. Move `<Focusable>`'s implementation into `<FocusScope>` so `<FocusScope>` is the leaf primitive.
-2. Reduce `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focusable.tsx` to a one-line re-export: `export { FocusScope as Focusable } from "./focus-scope"`. (Type alias if needed: `export type FocusableProps = FocusScopeProps`.)
-3. Add a `@deprecated` JSDoc comment on the re-export pointing at `<FocusScope>` and the eventual deletion card `01KQ5PSMYE...`.
-
-The actual file deletion happens in card `01KQ5PSMYE...` AFTER every per-component card lands. That card runs **last** in the chain, with `depends_on` set to every per-component card + the release-blocker umbrella.
-
-## What changes here
+## What changes

 ### React side

-- **Move `<Focusable>` impl into `<FocusScope>`.** All the work currently in `<Focusable>` (mint SpatialKey, call `spatial_register_focusable` â†’ renamed `spatial_register_scope`, render `<FocusIndicator>`, handle click â†’ `spatial_focus`, subscribe to claim events) lives in `<FocusScope>` directly.
-- **`<FocusScope>` becomes the leaf primitive** â€” no composite, no `kind` prop.
-- **`<Focusable>` becomes a thin re-export** of `<FocusScope>` for transitional compatibility. NOT deleted in this card.
-- **`<FocusZone>` keeps its current shape**; only `Focusable` references in its docstrings/types get renamed.
+- **Delete `<Focusable>`.** Its responsibilities (mint SpatialKey, call `spatial_register_focusable`, render `<FocusIndicator>`, handle click â†’ `spatial_focus`, subscribe to claim events) move into `<FocusScope>` directly.
+- **`<FocusScope>` becomes the leaf primitive** (no longer composite, no longer `kind` prop).
+- **`<FocusZone>` keeps its current shape** â€” it's already the container primitive, no changes beyond renaming any `Focusable` references in its docstrings/types.
 - **`<FocusLayer>` keeps its current shape**.
-- **Drop `kind="zone"` prop on `<FocusScope>`.** Call sites that need a zone use `<FocusZone>` directly. The transitional re-export of `<Focusable>` does not accept `kind="zone"` (it forwards to `<FocusScope>` which is now leaf-only).
+- All `<Focusable>` call sites become `<FocusScope>` call sites â€” but most of them already say `<FocusScope>` in the codebase, so this is mostly a "stop the indirection" change rather than a rename across hundreds of files.
+- `kind="zone"` prop on `<FocusScope>` goes away. Use `<FocusZone>` directly when a zone is wanted.

 ### Rust side

@@ -53,20 +42,17 @@

 Every card in the spatial-nav project that names `Focusable` or describes the composite `FocusScope` model needs a documentation refresh. Canonical terminology section in card `01KNQXW7HH...` is the source of truth and gets updated first. Sweep the others mechanically.

-## Why the bridging-as-deprecation pattern matters
-
-Per-component cards are blocked behind this one. They each TDD their visible-focus integration test. If `<Focusable>` disappears entirely here, every per-component card has to do a search-and-replace across its own files BEFORE it can red-green its actual test. That's lots of churn that bury the real work. With `<Focusable>` aliased to `<FocusScope>`, the per-component cards can:
+## Why this fixes the focus-not-visible bug

-- Switch their imports to `<FocusScope>` directly when convenient, OR
-- Leave them alone (the alias keeps everything compiling) and let the cleanup card sweep the imports later.
+Right now: the composite `<FocusScope>` wraps `<Focusable>`, so a leaf in the codebase actually mounts two stacked components. Each has its own click handler, its own `data-focused` attribute, its own indicator-render decision. When `showFocusBar={false}` is set on the outer composite, it suppresses the *composite's* indicator but the inner `<Focusable>`'s indicator may also be configured separately. The visual result depends on which layer "wins," which depends on subtle prop-flow decisions that vary by call site.

-Either way, the per-component card's TDD loop never breaks because of a missing primitive.
+After the collapse: one component, one click handler, one `data-focused`, one indicator render decision. Easy to reason about, easy to test "click â†’ indicator visible" end-to-end.

 ## Subtasks

 - [ ] Inventory every place `<Focusable>` is imported or referenced in `kanban-app/ui/src/`
 - [ ] Move `<Focusable>`'s implementation into `<FocusScope>` (focus-scope.tsx)
-- [ ] Reduce `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focusable.tsx` to: `export { FocusScope as Focusable } from "./focus-scope";` plus a `@deprecated` doc comment
+- [ ] Delete `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focusable.tsx`
 - [ ] Drop the `kind` prop from `<FocusScope>`; call sites that need a zone use `<FocusZone>` directly
 - [ ] Rename Rust `Focusable` struct â†’ `FocusScope` (in `swissarmyhammer-focus`)
 - [ ] Drop the Rust `FocusScope` enum (was sum of `Focusable | Zone`); registry stores two distinct types internally
@@ -74,34 +60,34 @@
 - [ ] Update terminology section in card `01KNQXW7HH...` to describe three peers, not four
 - [ ] Sweep every spatial-nav card for `Focusable` references; update doc references
 - [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` + `pnpm vitest run` â€” all green
+- [ ] Manual verification: clicking each kind of leaf (column header, card title, status pill, field label, navbar button, perspective tab, grid cell) shows a visible focus indicator

 ## Acceptance Criteria

 - [ ] `<FocusZone>` is the container primitive (unchanged)
 - [ ] `<FocusLayer>` is the layer primitive (unchanged)
-- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focusable.tsx` exists but is a thin re-export of `FocusScope` with `@deprecated` JSDoc
-- [ ] Rust `Focusable` struct renamed to `FocusScope`; no public enum; `spatial_register_focusable` â†’ `spatial_register_scope`
-- [ ] All existing tests pass after the rename + collapse â€” if a test references the old `<Focusable>` it still works because of the re-export
-- [ ] No breaking change to call sites: every existing `<Focusable>` usage continues to compile and behave identically
+- [ ] All seven leaf categories (column header, card title, card status, card pills, inspector label, inspector pill, navbar button, perspective tab, grid cell) show a visible focus indicator on click
+- [ ] All existing tests pass after the rename + collapse
